title: Custom Emulsions via CSV File
author: Opentrons
description: This protocol performs custom emulsions via two uploaded csv files - one for the aqueous solutions (slot 10) and another for the oil solutions (slot 11). Touch tips and delays are instilled after aspirations, and blowouts are instilled after dispensing from the top of the well. One liquid solution is dispensed at a time into as many rows that are provided in the csv. If a value of 0 is passed in the csv, the pipette will skip that well. For volumes less than 300ul, the P300 single channel pipette is used, otherwise for volumes 300-1000ul, the P1000 pipette is used. All aspirations and dispenses are in order of row for all racks and plates. * The csv should be formatted like so. Note that it should include the header, as well as a second row describing the initial volume in each tube in milliliters to ensure liquid height tracking, and avoid submerging the pipette plunger in solution. The aqueous and oil csv files do not necessarily need to have the same number of columns (liquid number), but they should have the same number of rows (up to 144 rows).
steps_raw: This protocol performs custom emulsions via two uploaded csv files - one for the aqueous solutions (slot 10) and another for the oil solutions (slot 11). Touch tips and delays are instilled after aspirations, and blowouts are instilled after dispensing from the top of the well. One liquid solution is dispensed at a time into as many rows that are provided in the csv. If a value of 0 is passed in the csv, the pipette will skip that well. For volumes less than 300ul, the P300 single channel pipette is used, otherwise for volumes 300-1000ul, the P1000 pipette is used. All aspirations and dispenses are in order of row for all racks and plates. * The csv should be formatted like so. Note that it should include the header, as well as a second row describing the initial volume in each tube in milliliters to ensure liquid height tracking, and avoid submerging the pipette plunger in solution. The aqueous and oil csv files do not necessarily need to have the same number of columns (liquid number), but they should have the same number of rows (up to 144 rows).
Aqueous solution is aspirated from the aqueous rack, with the volume informed by the csv.
Aqueous solution is dispensed from the top of the well by row into the 48 well plate.
Steps 1-3 repeated for the oil solution.
materials_raw: Opentrons 6 Tube Rack with Falcon 50 mL Conical
Opentrons P1000 Single Channel Electronic Pipette (GEN2)
Opentrons P300 Single Channel Electronic Pipette (GEN2)
